ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. SPECIALLY APPEARING GREG WAGNER'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. THE SERVICE OF SUMMONS ON MR. WAGNER WAS IMPROPER BECAUSE HE IS NOT A PARTY TO THE ACTION. (SEE FUSS V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (1958) 162 CAL.APP.2D 643, 646 ["ONE 'WHO IS NOT NAMED EITHER BY HIS TRUE OR A FICTITIOUS NAME OR AS AN UNKNOWN DEFENDANT IS NOT A PROPER PARTY TO AN ACTION, AND SERVICE OF SUMMONS UPON SUCH PERSON UPON PROPER MOTION SHOULD BE QUASHED.'"]). ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Apr-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan Stevens, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons.

Specially appearing Greg Wagner's motion to quash service of summons is granted. The service of summons on Mr. Wagner was improper because he is not a party to the action. (See Fuss v. City of Los Angeles (1958) 162 Cal.App.2d 643, 646 ["one 'who is not named either by his true or a fictitious name or as an unknown defendant is not a proper party to an action, and service of summons upon such person upon proper motion should be quashed.'"]).

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
